symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic bilateral varicose veins with dependent aching relieved by elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Varicosities are the substrate in which superficial thrombophlebitis develops.
Relief with elevation is a venous pattern; arterial pain is worsened by elevation.

symptoms.symptom_name: New tender, firm, erythematous cord-like segment over a left calf varicosity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable tender erythematous cord following a superficial vein is the defining feature of superficial thrombophlebitis.
Linear erythema tracking a vein is more focal than the spreading plaque of cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus and inflamed skin over the affected vein

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Local inflammation of the vein wall produces overlying skin irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever or systemic illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile status argues against cellulitis, though it does not exclude early or localized infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of massive whole-calf swelling and deep calf pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces DVT probability but is not sufficient to exclude it — DVT can be present without dramatic swelling and requires duplex to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain, breathlessness, or haemoptysis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current PE features, but their absence does not exclude an underlying DVT that could later embolise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Venous eczema and haemosiderin (brown) staining around the ankles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Skin changes confirm chronic venous insufficiency, the underlying substrate; video-observable so admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable warm, firm, cord-like thrombosed superficial vein on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician palpation of a warm firm cord confirms superficial thrombophlebitis — sealed clinician finding, unavailable to the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative Homan's sign, palpable pedal pulses, no pitting oedema to the knee

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person exam findings lower DVT probability; unavailable via telehealth, which is why the AI must escalate for hands-on assessment.
Strong palpable pedal pulses exclude significant arterial disease — a clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Venous duplex ultrasound of the lower limb

investigation_details.expected_result: Non-compressible thrombosed superficial vein segment; assessment of proximity to the saphenofemoral junction; patent deep veins excluding DVT
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Clot extent and junctional proximity remain unknown and a concurrent DVT is not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Duplex confirms superficial thrombosis, maps its extent, and is the definitive test to exclude concurrent DVT.
Duplex is the standard investigation to confirm or exclude DVT — impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A tender erythematous cord arising within a known varicosity is highly specific for superficial thrombophlebitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms superficial thrombophlebitis, defines clot extent and proximity to the saphenofemoral junction, and excludes concurrent DVT.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical antibiotics or blood cultures for presumed cellulitis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The patient is afebrile with linear erythema tracking a vein — this is not cellulitis, and antibiotics are not indicated.
